title: The Port of Alicante Launches the Tender for Its First Renewable Hydrogen Plant and Reinforces Its Green Roadmap
content: The Port Authority of Alicante has approved the call for public tenders for the construction and operation of a renewable hydrogen production and supply plant at Pier 19, a key project in its commitment to renewable energies and the reduction of the carbon footprint of the port area. This initiative is part of the roadmap promoted by its president, Luis RodrÃ­guez, aimed at turning the port into a smart, green, innovative and competitive energy node in the Mediterranean. The plant is part of the PORTALI-H2 project and represents a decisive advance in the integration of clean industrial technologies, with the aim of improving energy efficiency and reducing emissions associated with both port operations and maritime and land transport linked to the port. The facility will make it possible to produce, store and supply renewable hydrogen from the site itself, facilitating the incorporation of more sustainable mobility and logistics solutions for ships, port machinery and land fleets. The tender for this plant gives concrete content to the commitment expressed by President Luis RodrÃ­guez to develop a port aimed at decarbonisation, in line with the European Green Deal and the strategic framework of the state port system. In this roadmap, green hydrogen, together with electrification and other renewable energies, is positioned as a central lever to progressively reduce the port's carbon footprint and strengthen its long-term competitiveness.
author: Analysts Account
